nodes.label: PhraseLoop
nodes.rationale: Helps Brazilian A2-B1 self-study learners turn real English and their mistakes into native-audio review cards on Mac. One Home-led loop first; speech generation, Anki export, Speak, plans, and provider setup stay behind the core loop.

nodes.label: Five-Second Promise
nodes.rationale: Turn real English and your mistakes into native-audio review cards in 2 minutes. The switching bet is speed: the path from source material to a reviewed card must be under 2 minutes with less friction than Anki, Migaku, or LingQ.

nodes.label: Native Source Audio Differentiator
nodes.rationale: Phrases come from real English the user chose and review cards keep that native audio, not a robotic re-read — something manual flashcard apps and generic chatbots don't do.

nodes.label: Mistakes Become Drills Differentiator
nodes.rationale: A correction isn't a dead-end note: each fix turns into a review card, and weak spots feed back into more practice.

nodes.label: Local Speech Runtime (Kokoro + Whisper)
nodes.rationale: Kokoro 1.0 via sherpa-onnx and whisper.cpp/Metal loaded directly by Node — no Python, no helper app, no auxiliary Dock icon. Models downloaded on first use, verified by SHA-256, installed atomically.

nodes.label: YouTube Audio Import Pipeline (yt-dlp + whisper.cpp)
nodes.rationale: Downloads audio only (no video) with yt-dlp because YouTube.js can no longer decipher stream URLs; transcribes locally with whisper.cpp producing a timestamped transcript. Transcription runs 100% locally.

nodes.label: Pluggable AI Providers
nodes.rationale: Ollama is the default; PhraseLoop never sends content to a cloud provider unless explicitly selected. OpenRouter, Claude, and GPT are opt-in via keys. First lesson and first review show no provider setup.
